title: Add AUTHORED schema contracts and backfill query definitions
description: Extend the graph schema for Phase 2 by adding the `AUTHORED.created_at` relationship index and centralizing the idempotent backfill/audit Cypher used to reconcile legacy `Human_Post.author_id` rows. Keep this row scoped to schema/bootstrap contracts and the tests that prove the new index becomes visible on clean boot.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the application boots with the Phase 2 schema change → THEN Neo4j creates `rhizodelta_authored_created_at_idx`; WHEN historical backfill is executed against posts whose `author_id` matches an existing `UserAccount` → THEN each post gains exactly one `AUTHORED` edge and reruns do not duplicate edges; WHEN consistency audit runs on posts missing the canonical edge → THEN it returns `post.node_id` plus `author_id` instead of fabricating placeholder users; ref: Phase 2 schema/index/backfill contract
review_initial_requirements: Follow design D1 and D3 exactly: `AUTHORED` is the canonical semantic while `author_id` remains a projection, and historical backfill must stay outside startup flow; keep backfill/audit Cypher centralized rather than scattering raw strings across unrelated services; schema/index initialization must stay idempotent with the existing `DatabaseInitializer` pattern.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must confirm old `author_id`-only readers still work after adding the new index and query definitions; no startup-time full historical migration may be introduced; rollback must remain possible by dropping only `rhizodelta_authored_created_at_idx` and deleting `AUTHORED` edges without touching `author_id`.

title: Persist AUTHORED during Human_Post creation
description: Update the human post write path so `PostService` writes `author_id` and `AUTHORED` in the same transaction for every newly created human-authored post. This row covers canonical write semantics, duplicate-delivery safety, and the explicit failure path when the author account cannot be resolved.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN an authenticated user creates a new `Human_Post` → THEN the persisted post keeps `author_id = JWT.sub` and exactly one `(:UserAccount)-[:AUTHORED {created_at=post.created_at}]->(:Human_Post)` edge is written in the same transaction; WHEN the same logical post is retried or replayed → THEN no duplicate `AUTHORED` edge is created; WHEN the write path cannot resolve the author account → THEN the operation fails explicitly and does not create a placeholder user node; ref: canonical author write path and duplicate-delivery scenarios
review_initial_requirements: Follow design D2: keep `author_id` and `AUTHORED` in one transaction inside `PostService.upsertByRequestId`, and source `AUTHORED.created_at` from the post creation timestamp rather than a separate clock read; do not move this to an async compensating step and do not introduce silent fallback behavior.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must prove existing `author_id` projection remains populated for old clients and downstream services; duplicate-delivery behavior must stay idempotent; rollback must allow old code to continue working from `author_id` alone even if Phase 2 code is removed.
notes: generated_from:openspec/changes/user-authored-edge; source:docs/user-domain-modeling-plan.md; picked_reason:UAE-02 先落地 Human_Post 双轨写入，把 author_id 与 AUTHORED 绑定在同一事务内，直接解锁后续读模型与回填验证; evidence:PostService 在 createHumanPost 事务内新增 author 存在性校验，并在帖子创建后同步写入 AUTHORED 边且沿用 createdAt 作为边时间; manual_test:timeout 300s mvn test -Dtest=PostServiceAuthoringContractTest,AsyncPostPipelineIntegrationTest; evidence:PostServiceAuthoringContractTest 1/1 通过，缺失 author 账户时显式抛出 author_id not found 且不会进入帖子 upsert; evidence:AsyncPostPipelineIntegrationTest 3/3 通过，验证 AUTHORED 成功写入、JWT subject 覆盖 forged author_id、重复投递后 AUTHORED 边仍为 1 条; risk:low 当前剩余风险主要是异步测试里 embedding/routing 分支仍会因假 API key 输出 Invalid token 日志，但不影响 UAE-02 要求的帖子持久化与 AUTHORED 语义; done_at:2026-04-22

title: Expose authenticated public author profile lookup
description: Add `GET /api/users/{user_id}/profile` so authenticated callers can resolve another user's author identifier into a public profile projection. Scope the row to the public-read API, fallback behavior for missing profiles, `UNAVAILABLE` masking for suspended/deleted accounts, and authorization enforcement.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN an authenticated caller requests `GET /api/users/{user_id}/profile` for an active account → THEN the response returns only `user_id`, `username`, `display_name`, and `avatar_url`; WHEN the account exists but has no `HAS_PROFILE` edge → THEN the response still succeeds with `display_name = username` and `avatar_url = null`; WHEN the target account status is `SUSPENDED` or `DELETED` → THEN the response returns `{ user_id, status: \UNAVAILABLE\" }`; WHEN no valid JWT is provided → THEN the endpoint returns HTTP 401; ref: public author profile scenarios"
review_initial_requirements: Follow design D4: reuse `UserAccount + UserProfile` and expose only public fields, never credentials or self-only settings; keep Phase 1 self-profile endpoints untouched and add a separate public-read contract instead of overloading `/api/users/me/profile`; honor the current decision that suspended and deleted accounts are both masked as `UNAVAILABLE`.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must verify `/api/auth/*` and `/api/users/me/profile` behavior remains unchanged; missing `UserProfile` must continue to fall back to username instead of returning 500; security review must confirm `SecurityConfig` still requires authentication for the new endpoint and no extra account details leak for unavailable users.
notes: generated_from:openspec/changes/user-authored-edge; source:docs/user-domain-modeling-plan.md; picked_reason:UAE-03 先落地公开 author profile 查询，可直接支撑 Phase 2 作者展示解析且改动面集中在用户画像 API; evidence:UserProfileController 新增 GET /api/users/{userId}/profile，active 用户返回公开字段，缺失 profile 时 display_name 回退 username，SUSPENDED/DELETED 统一返回 UNAVAILABLE; manual_test:timeout 300s mvn test -Dtest=UserProfileApiIntegrationTest; evidence:UserProfileApiIntegrationTest 10/10 通过，新增覆盖公开 profile 成功、fallback、UNAVAILABLE、无 JWT 401 四个场景，并保留 /me/profile 既有回归; risk:low 当前剩余风险主要是 NodeQueryService 和前端仍未消费这个公开 profile 接口，这部分属于 UAE-04；本行已把公开资料读口稳定下来; done_at:2026-04-22

title: Add author display projections to node query payloads
description: Extend NodeQueryService and NodeQueryController so node payloads can include optional `author_username` and `author_display_name` while preserving `author_id`. Scope this row to backend read-model enrichment and API payload shape only.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN a node query returns a human-authored node whose author has `UserAccount` and `UserProfile` → THEN the payload includes `author_id`, `author_username`, and `author_display_name`; WHEN the profile lacks `display_name` → THEN `author_display_name` falls back to `username`; WHEN callers ignore the new fields → THEN payloads remain backward compatible because `author_id` is still present; ref: backend author projection scenarios
review_initial_requirements: Follow design D5: keep current read paths centered on `author_id` and add optional display projections without rewriting all topology queries around AUTHORED traversal; prefer a centralized enrichment path over copy-pasting OPTIONAL MATCH logic into every large Cypher block; NodeQueryController remains the single payload boundary.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must confirm all existing `/api/nodes/*` consumers still receive `author_id`; payloads for AI/System nodes must remain valid when no author projections exist; do not remove or rename existing JSON fields while adding `author_username` / `author_display_name`.

title: Implement historical AUTHORED backfill and consistency audit
description: Implement the executable path for Phase 2 historical reconciliation so legacy `Human_Post.author_id` data can be converted into canonical `AUTHORED` edges and audited for drift. Keep the row focused on batchable backfill behavior, failure-sample visibility, and re-run safety rather than on startup schema initialization.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the Phase 2 backfill runs on historical `Human_Post` rows with non-null `author_id` and matching `UserAccount.user_id` → THEN each row gains exactly one `AUTHORED` edge; WHEN a post references an author that cannot be resolved → THEN the process records a failure sample or audit result instead of creating a placeholder user; WHEN the same backfill runs a second time → THEN it does not create duplicate edges; WHEN the consistency audit runs → THEN it reports missing `AUTHORED` edges by `post.node_id` and `author_id`; ref: backfill and audit scenarios
review_initial_requirements: Follow design D3: historical reconciliation is an explicit post-deploy task, not a startup migration; keep the backfill idempotent and observable, and choose a task entrypoint or script location that is easy to audit; never auto-create missing accounts just to satisfy edge creation.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must verify new-post writes do not depend on historical backfill having already run; old `author_id` payloads must remain valid during and after backfill; operational review must confirm the audit output is sufficient to repair drift manually and that reruns are safe.

title: Build Phase 2 integration verification suite
description: Add the automated verification baseline for Phase 2 so the new author edge, public profile API, and projection changes are guarded by repeatable tests. This row is limited to test code and apply-readiness checks, not to runbook authoring.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the Phase 2 integration suite runs → THEN it verifies new posts persist both `author_id` and `AUTHORED`; WHEN public profile API tests run → THEN normal, fallback, `UNAVAILABLE`, and unauthenticated cases are all covered; WHEN read-model and backfill regression tests run → THEN duplicate-edge prevention and old-client compatibility are exercised; WHEN apply-readiness is checked → THEN the OpenSpec change remains consistent with implemented behavior and test coverage; ref: Phase 2 automated verification tasks
review_initial_requirements: Keep tests aligned with the spec scenarios rather than with incidental implementation details; prefer existing project patterns for integration tests and keep validation deterministic where possible; ensure the suite proves both canonical-edge behavior and compatibility with `author_id`-only readers.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must confirm tests cover old-client compatibility, missing-profile fallback, and duplicate-delivery behavior; do not claim manual operational drills as automated evidence; if environment limits any part of the suite, record that limitation explicitly rather than silently skipping coverage.

title: Document Phase 2 runbook and rollback path

acceptance_criteria: WHEN the Phase 2 runbook is published → THEN it documents the historical `AUTHORED` backfill script, the consistency audit query, and the rollback command set for removing `AUTHORED` edges plus `rhizodelta_authored_created_at_idx`; WHEN operators consult the runbook after deployment → THEN they can distinguish normal post-deploy backfill from startup schema initialization; WHEN rollback guidance is read → THEN it is clear that `author_id` remains intact and old code can continue to function from the projection alone; ref: Phase 2 migration and rollback documentation
review_initial_requirements: Follow the design migration plan and rollback strategy exactly, and keep the docs framed in operational terms rather than file-specific implementation trivia; cross-reference the Phase 2 source design or the broader domain plan instead of duplicating long design prose; prefer the existing `docs/runbooks/` style used by prior user-domain changes.
review_regression_requirements: Regression review must verify the runbook stays aligned if backfill or rollback commands change later; documentation must not imply startup-time historical migration when the design explicitly rejects it; rollback wording must preserve the invariant that old clients continue to work from `author_id` even if `AUTHORED` is removed.
